metadata.title: Triton Safety Instrumented System Attack Scenario

Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Sector Complex The attackers behind the Triton Safety Instrumented System Attack aim to disrupt the safety instrumented systems in the healthcare and public health sector. They employ a multi-stage attack plan involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets File Process Scheduled Task Malware Artifact Application Log Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) User Account Public IP/Network Block Network Share DNS Record Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method File Metadata (Mechanism) Reg.exe Utility Command Execution (Mechanism) Scheduled Task/Job Reg.exe Utility Application Log Content (Mechanism) SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking User Account Authentication (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Network Share Access (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about the healthcare and public health sector by utilizing a resource leak on the target to deplete the quantity of the resource available to service legitimate requests.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers identify potential vulnerabilities in the network infrastructure by monitoring network traffic between nodes of a public or multicast network in an attempt to capture sensitive information at the protocol level.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers develop a custom malware similar to Triton to evade detection.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers target specific employees with phishing emails to gain initial access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers compromise employee devices through drive-by downloads.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-663: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution
stage.description: The attackers execute the malware on compromised systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers schedule tasks to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers maintain persistence through scheduled tasks.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to escalate privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.technique_name: T1198: SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking
stage.description: The attackers disable antivirus software to avoid detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers use brute force to obtain credentials.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers map the network to identify sensitive areas.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers scan network services to identify vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attackers use valid accounts to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect data from local systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.technique_name: T1039: Data from Network Shared Drive
stage.capec: CAPEC-643: Identify Shared Files/Directories on System
stage.description: The attackers collect data from network traffic.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate data over alternative protocols.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers cause denial of service to disrupt operations.

Scenario.summary: The Triton Safety Instrumented System Attack is a complex attack that involves multiple stages, including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact. The attackers use various techniques to achieve their goals, including spearphishing, drive-by downloads, user execution, and brute force attacks.
